question_text: An anti-static mat used in an electronics workshop has a resistance of 1 MΩ (1,000,000 Ω). A technician with a static charge of 10,000 V stands on the mat. Calculate the current that flows through the mat to discharge the static electricity. (Hint: Use I = V ÷ R)
explanation: Using Ohm's law: I = V ÷ R = 10,000 V ÷ 1,000,000 Ω = 0.01 A. Anti-static mats safely ground static charges by allowing them to flow harmlessly to earth at a controlled, low current rate rather than causing a dangerous spark.
